title: Fix the rate limiter blocking users forever
description:
# Issue #61 - Rate limiter never resets and mixes clients together

After the first 5 requests our API returns 429 forever - even the next day. Worse, one noisy client exhausts the limit for EVERY client.

RateLimiter is supposed to be a sliding-window limiter: at most max_requests per window_seconds, per client.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- each client_id gets its own budget
- requests older than window_seconds no longer count
- the injected clock (self.clock) is used for all time arithmetic so it stays testable
optimal_hint: Ideal fix: dict mapping client_id to a deque of timestamps; on allow(), prune entries older than now - window_seconds, then compare the count to max_requests. Watch for solutions that reset the whole window at once (fixed window) instead of sliding.
